text:
Once there were three brothers: Arthur, Thomas, and Alfred. They lived in a quaint little village in England. Their father had passed away years ago, leaving them his modest cottage on the edge of the woods.

One sunny afternoon, after finishing their chores, Arthur suggested they venture into the ancient forest behind their house. The brothers had always been fascinated by these woods; tales of brave knights hiding creatures passed down through generations filled their imagination. Today, they were determined to uncover the legendary treasure rumored to exist within.

As they ventured deeper into the shadows, the trees began to close in around them. Alfred, the eldest, suggested they look for signs of a compass. There were gnarled trees here, sure, but nothing like the needle Arthur pulled out of his father’s old compass box.

They had walked for hours when suddenly Thomas stumbled across something shiny. It was a small, ornate box. Each brother reached for it. Arthur claimed it belonged to their father. Thomas didn't believe
